meta.phase: CAPADEX 3.0 · Program 2 · Phase 2.5 — Observability, Monitoring & Operational Readiness
meta.honesty: Coverage ⟂ Confidence ⟂ Adoption are SEPARATE and NEVER composited. The 10 operational axes are certified independently and never combined. null ≠ 0. Structural coverage is not a runtime/quality/adoption claim. Built ≠ Operated ≠ Recoverable.

axes.label: Observability
axes.definition: Every critical service exposes a health/readiness/liveness/status signal that is measurable.

axes.label: Monitoring
axes.definition: Live monitors compose service/runtime/background-job health into a continuous operational picture.

axes.label: Logging
axes.definition: Structured logs carry request/correlation identity and mask sensitive data.

axes.label: Metrics
axes.definition: Latency/resource/AI/KPI signals are MEASURED (APM/export gaps reported as honest NULL).

axes.label: Alerting
axes.definition: Failure conditions are detectable; alert-rule/notification wiring is measured (not assumed).

axes.label: AI Operations
axes.definition: AI prompt/provider/model/latency/retry are observable (cost/token tracking reported honestly).

axes.label: Assessment Operations
axes.definition: Assessment start/complete/abandon lifecycle is traceable from persisted session state.

axes.label: Report Operations
axes.definition: Report request/generate/export/delivery is traceable from persisted report state.

axes.label: Disaster Recovery
axes.definition: Backup/restore/RTO/RPO validation (largely infra-owned — measured honestly, not assumed).

axes.label: Operational Readiness
axes.definition: Operational dashboards + integration monitoring exist so the platform can be operated/supported.

domains.label: Service & API Observability
domains.note: Health (/api/health), readiness (/api/health/ready) + a 6-domain aggregator exist. A dedicated /metrics export endpoint and a /version endpoint are NOT present — honest gaps, not fabricated.

domains.label: Monitoring Coverage
domains.note: Runtime Intelligence + the 6-domain health aggregator + global-monitoring compose a live picture. Trend/drift is snapshot-based (explicit capture) — with <2 snapshots, trend is honestly unavailable.

domains.label: Background Jobs & Queue
domains.note: An in-process fire-and-forget event bus exists. A durable queue, a Dead-Letter-Queue, and per-job retry/failure persistence are NOT present — honest gaps (a durable-queue/DLQ is a Medium operational gap, never fabricated).

domains.label: Logging & Traceability
domains.note: A levelled logger (debug/warn/error), a per-request requestId, redaction-at-write, and a redacted admin audit trail exist. Distributed trace-IDs propagated Node→FastAPI and a structured-log pipeline (APM) are NOT present — honest gaps.

domains.label: Metrics Coverage
domains.note: DB latency, event-loop lag, process/OS memory + CPU, AI-runtime rows and a KPI daily rollup are MEASURED. A metrics-export endpoint (Prometheus/statsd) + API-throughput/error-rate counters + cache-hit ratio are NOT present — honest NULL (DEFERRED), never estimated.

domains.label: Alerting
domains.note: Failure CONDITIONS are detectable (health domains report down/degraded; global-monitoring derives status). A durable alert-RULE store + notification routing (email/pager/webhook) are NOT present — alerts are client-derived from status, not pushed. Honest Medium gap.

domains.label: AI Operations
domains.note: AI health (checkAIHealth), provider/model, latency and retry behaviour are observable + persisted to ai_runtime_monitoring. Per-request COST and TOKEN accounting are NOT tracked — honest gap (never fabricated as 0).

domains.label: Assessment Operations
domains.note: Assessment lifecycle (in_progress/completed) is persisted in capadex_sessions and is fully traceable. Explicit per-question/section TIMING telemetry is partial. Completion rate is derivable at read-time; reported as Coverage, with real volume as a SEPARATE Adoption axis.

domains.label: Report Operations
domains.note: Report generation + a report-pack builder + Zoho email delivery exist; report state is persisted in capadex_reports. Explicit generation-TIME + download-status telemetry is partial — honest Coverage, real volume as a SEPARATE Adoption axis.

domains.label: Integration Monitoring
domains.note: External-AI health (checkAIHealth), a safety circuit-breaker, Zoho email and Razorpay payment integration exist. A unified integration-health dashboard with per-integration auth-status + timeout + rate-limit telemetry is partial — honest gap.

domains.label: Disaster Recovery
domains.note: Managed-database backups are infra-owned (Cloud SQL / provider), NOT validated in-repo. Restore drills, documented recovery procedures, and measured RTO/RPO are NOT present in the repository — reported as an honest DEAD_END/gap (infra-owned), never fabricated as validated.

domains.label: Operational Dashboards
domains.note: A super-admin console + a live 6-domain health view + mission-control aggregators provide operations/AI/assessment/security surfaces. Dedicated executive + infrastructure operational dashboards are partial — honest Coverage.

decisions.decision: COMPOSE the existing observability substrate into ONE read-only operational-readiness view. NO parallel/duplicate monitoring engine, telemetry pipeline, or metadata store was created.

This phase MEASURES operational coverage and CLASSIFIES gaps. Deeper enhancements (DLQ, token/cost accounting, metrics export, alert-rule store, DR drills) are recorded as classified gaps for a future task — NOT built here, to preserve byte-identical-OFF and avoid business/infra change.
The 10 operational axes are certified SEPARATELY and are NEVER combined into a single number. The overall verdict is a SEPARATE structural axis, not an average.
Coverage (evidence exists) is reported separately from Adoption (real non-demo volume). Real operational volume is honest-low/0 in a dev environment — a usage axis, never a gap.

certification.honesty_note: These are 10 SEPARATE structural-coverage scores (0–100 or NULL). They are NEVER combined into a single number. Structural coverage means evidence EXISTS — it is NOT a runtime, quality, or adoption claim. null ≠ 0.

adoption.note: Adoption = real persisted volume per operational domain. Reported SEPARATELY from Coverage/Certification and NEVER composited. In a dev environment real operational volume is honest-low/0 — a usage axis, never an engineering gap. null = unreadable (≠ 0 = empty).

open_gaps.title: No metrics-export endpoint / APM pipeline
open_gaps.detail: API throughput/error-rate counters, cache-hit ratio and a Prometheus/statsd export are absent. Latency/resource/AI/KPI signals ARE measured; the missing piece is external export/aggregation. Deferred (infra-owned).

open_gaps.title: No durable queue / Dead-Letter-Queue for background jobs
open_gaps.detail: The event bus is in-process fire-and-forget with no persisted retry/DLQ. Failed async work is not durably tracked. A durable queue + DLQ is a Medium operational enhancement for a future task.

open_gaps.title: No alert-rule store / notification routing
open_gaps.detail: Failure conditions are detectable but alerts are client-derived from status, not pushed via a durable rule store + notification channel. Medium operational gap.

open_gaps.title: No AI cost / token accounting
open_gaps.detail: AI latency/retry/health/provider/model are observable but per-request cost and token usage are not tracked. Medium gap; never fabricated as 0.

open_gaps.title: No cross-service correlation-ID propagation (Node→FastAPI) / distributed tracing
open_gaps.detail: A per-request requestId exists on the Node service but is not propagated to the FastAPI upload service, and there is no distributed-tracing backend. Low/Medium gap.

open_gaps.title: No /version and no /metrics endpoint
open_gaps.detail: Health + readiness exist; a build/version-info endpoint and a machine-readable metrics endpoint are absent. Low gap.

open_gaps.title: Disaster-recovery validation is infra-owned, not validated in-repo
open_gaps.detail: Managed-DB backups exist at the infra layer but restore drills, documented recovery procedures and measured RTO/RPO are not present in the repository. Future/infra gap — reported honestly, never claimed as validated.

resolved_gaps.detail: A 6-domain live health monitor + /api/health + /api/health/ready + snapshot history already exist and are composed here (no new health engine).
Live runtime/resource/service monitoring + command-center global-monitoring already exist and are composed here.
Per-request identity, redaction-at-write, and a redacted audit trail already exist and are composed here.
AI-runtime + orchestration-performance persistence + a KPI daily rollup already exist and are measured here.
External-AI health probe + AI-runtime persistence already exist and are composed here.
A circuit-breaker for external calls + a super-admin operational console already exist and are composed here.

Medium.title: No metrics-export endpoint / APM pipeline
Medium.detail: API throughput/error-rate counters, cache-hit ratio and a Prometheus/statsd export are absent. Latency/resource/AI/KPI signals ARE measured; the missing piece is external export/aggregation. Deferred (infra-owned).

Medium.title: No durable queue / Dead-Letter-Queue for background jobs
Medium.detail: The event bus is in-process fire-and-forget with no persisted retry/DLQ. Failed async work is not durably tracked. A durable queue + DLQ is a Medium operational enhancement for a future task.

Medium.title: No alert-rule store / notification routing
Medium.detail: Failure conditions are detectable but alerts are client-derived from status, not pushed via a durable rule store + notification channel. Medium operational gap.

Medium.title: No AI cost / token accounting
Medium.detail: AI latency/retry/health/provider/model are observable but per-request cost and token usage are not tracked. Medium gap; never fabricated as 0.

Low.title: No cross-service correlation-ID propagation (Node→FastAPI) / distributed tracing
Low.detail: A per-request requestId exists on the Node service but is not propagated to the FastAPI upload service, and there is no distributed-tracing backend. Low/Medium gap.

Low.title: No /version and no /metrics endpoint
Low.detail: Health + readiness exist; a build/version-info endpoint and a machine-readable metrics endpoint are absent. Low gap.

Future.title: Disaster-recovery validation is infra-owned, not validated in-repo
Future.detail: Managed-DB backups exist at the infra layer but restore drills, documented recovery procedures and measured RTO/RPO are not present in the repository. Future/infra gap — reported honestly, never claimed as validated.

registry_present.note: Canonical operational-readiness registry present (12 domains across 10 axes).

no_new_monitoring_system.note: COMPOSES the existing observability substrate. No parallel/duplicate monitoring engine, telemetry pipeline, or metadata store was created.

read_only_no_ddl.note: Read paths are GET-only, never-throws, and create ZERO tables. The only write path is an explicit POST snapshot capture (flag-ON) that owns its lazy ensure-schema.

axes_never_composited.note: The 10 operational axes are certified SEPARATELY. The verdict is a SEPARATE structural axis, not an average.

no_business_logic_change.note: No assessment/AI/report/workflow logic changed. Additive + flag-gated; flag OFF is byte-identical incl. schema.

no_dormant_activation.note: No flag flipped for another subsystem; nothing dormant activated. Engines read by existence/persisted-output, never invoked.

validation.honesty_note: STRUCTURAL_VALIDATED = the operational-readiness composer is built, reuses the existing observability substrate, and preserves compatibility. It is NOT a runtime/outcome/adoption claim. Built ≠ Operated ≠ Monitored ≠ Recoverable.

summary.phase: CAPADEX 3.0 · Program 2 · Phase 2.5 — Observability, Monitoring & Operational Readiness

enterprise_ready.note: CAPADEX can be observed, monitored and operated at a STRUCTURAL level: every certified axis composes existing substrate with 0 Launch-Critical gaps. Enterprise production-operation CONFIDENCE is WITHHELD (a SEPARATE axis) pending real operational volume + the classified Medium/Low/Future gaps (metrics export, DLQ, alert-rule store, AI cost/token, correlation-ID propagation, DR drills). Coverage⟂Confidence⟂Adoption never composited.

summary.adoption_note: Adoption = real persisted volume per operational domain. Reported SEPARATELY from Coverage/Certification and NEVER composited. In a dev environment real operational volume is honest-low/0 — a usage axis, never an engineering gap. null = unreadable (≠ 0 = empty).

decisions.decision: COMPOSE the existing observability substrate into ONE read-only operational-readiness view. NO parallel/duplicate monitoring engine, telemetry pipeline, or metadata store was created.

This phase MEASURES operational coverage and CLASSIFIES gaps. Deeper enhancements (DLQ, token/cost accounting, metrics export, alert-rule store, DR drills) are recorded as classified gaps for a future task — NOT built here, to preserve byte-identical-OFF and avoid business/infra change.
The 10 operational axes are certified SEPARATELY and are NEVER combined into a single number. The overall verdict is a SEPARATE structural axis, not an average.
Coverage (evidence exists) is reported separately from Adoption (real non-demo volume). Real operational volume is honest-low/0 in a dev environment — a usage axis, never a gap.

summary.axes_note: Coverage ⟂ Confidence ⟂ Adoption are SEPARATE and NEVER composited. The 10 operational axes are certified independently and never combined. null ≠ 0. Built ≠ Operated ≠ Recoverable.
